taskVersion.name: epu_q2_short
taskVersion.definition:
@kbench.task(name="epu_q2_short")
def epu_q2_short(
    llm,
    article_key: str,
    content_for_eval: str,
    gold_epu: int,
    short_pre: str = "",
    short_post: str = "",
    medium_pre: str = "",
    medium_post: str = "",
    long_pre: str = "",
    long_post: str = "",
    disagreement_flag: int = 0,
) -> float:
    out = model_json_call(llm, build_q2_prompt(content_for_eval, short_pre, short_post))
    m = binary_match(out["epu"], gold_epu)
    return 0.0 if m is None else float(m)

parts.text:
You are evaluating only the text between TARGET ARTICLE START and TARGET ARTICLE END.

Task:
Decide whether the TARGET ARTICLE expresses ECONOMIC POLICY UNCERTAINTY (EPU).

Labeling rule:
- EPU = 1 only if the TARGET ARTICLE discusses uncertainty about government policy,
  policy decisions, possible policy actions, political outcomes affecting policy,
  or the economic effects of policy.
- EPU = 0 for general economics, elections, markets, war, uncertainty, or foreign events
  unless the uncertainty is specifically tied to economic policy.

IMPORTANT:
- Use only the text inside TARGET ARTICLE START and TARGET ARTICLE END for the decision.
- Return ONLY valid JSON.
- No markdown. No explanation. No code fence.

Return exactly these keys:
{
  "epu": 0 or 1,
  "confidence": number between 0 and 1,
  "focus_excerpt": "short quote from target article only"
}

Archi

TARGET ARTICLE START
Home of the San Francisco Chronicle Subscribe to the weekend Chronicle John Diaz Sunday, March 22, 2009 Over the past month, ever since the Hearst Corp. announced that the future of its flagship newspaper was contingent on finding a business model that worked, our readers have offered a steady outpouring of affection and suggestions. Everyone seems to have a solution. "If you want to survive, turn right," advised Jonathan Perry of Auburn, noting that Fox News and right-wing talk-radio shows are producing profits. "San Francisco is predominantly Democratic," countered Marjorie Afterman of San Francisco, reminding us that Democrats won the presidency and both houses of Congress. "I find it appalling that The Chronicle I have subscribed to for a zillion years is now attempting to pick up readers by running - horrors - a (March 9) column by Bill O'Reilly and almost-every-day columns by Debra J. Saunders." Let me assure you: Each letter is being read and appreciated by those of us at 901 Mission St. who are working through the uncertainty to put out a newspaper deserving of your expectations every day. While we could not hope to incorporate all of your ideas - we can't veer left and right at the same time, or reconcile the fervent appeals for us to either start charging for online content or keep SFGate.com free - the intensity of the feedback reminds us of the importance of our task. I had to smile when I read a letter from a reader from San Rafael who proclaimed, "The end is nea
